core_mechanism: Complete classification of the irreducible representations of a graded algebraic structure by decomposing each representation into simultaneous eigenspaces (weight spaces) indexed by a character lattice and proving that, under a finiteness constraint on eigenspace dimensions, only a short exhaustive list of canonical representation families can occur
brief_reason: The abstract shows every irreducible weight module with finite weight multiplicities over the algebra falls into exactly one of two canonical families (intermediate-series or finitely-dense), which is precisely an eigenspace/weight-space decomposition combined with an exhaustive finite-multiplicity classification argument.
